business.headline: What happens if Indians stop buying gold for a year like PM Modi asked?Prime Minister Narendra Modi has urged Indians to postpone non-essential gold purchases amid rising oil prices and West Asia tensions. Economists say any sustained fall in demand could ease pressure on the current account deficit, rupee and forex reserves.

PM Modi's call hits gold prices: Should investors worry?For years, gold has been the go-to safe investment for Indian families during uncertain times. But after PM Modi's call to cut back on gold buying, prices took a hit. As global worries rise and the yellow metal weakens, should investors start worrying?

The math behind PM Modi's gold, petrol and foreign travel advisoryPM Modi's latest advisory is not just about cutting fuel use or avoiding gold purchases. It is really about India's current account deficit, rising crude oil prices and protecting the economy from external shocks. Here is why your fuel bill, gold jewellery and foreign trip suddenly matter more than you think.

Titan shares fall over 6%: Why jewellery stocks are under pressure todayTitan and other jewellery stocks fell sharply after Prime Minister Narendra Modi urged people to avoid buying gold for weddings for a year. The remark added to concerns over gold demand, imports and pressure on the rupee amid elevated crude prices.

Sensex crashes 1,000 points: Why is the stock market falling today?The selloff on Dalal Street was broad-based across sectors, with banking, aviation, jewellery and consumption-linked stocks witnessing heavy pressure amid concerns over rising crude oil prices and their impact on India's economy.
Sensex plunges 800 points at open as crude oil jumps on US-Iran tensionsThe S&P BSE Sensex was down 847.62 points to 76,480.57, while the NSE Nifty50 lost 249.65 points to 23,926.50 as of 9:27 am.

Will stock market crash today after PM Modi's warning on fuel and gold buying?Markets are expected to react to Prime Minister Narendra Modi's remarks asking citizens to conserve fuel and avoid buying gold for weddings for one year amid pressure from rising global energy prices.

Why PM Modi asked Indian families not to buy gold for a yearPM Modi asked families to avoid buying gold for weddings for a year. The appeal linked rising oil prices and gold imports to pressure on the rupee and foreign exchange reserves.

Rs 5 crore, Rs 12 crore, Rs 40 crore? What retirement means for Indians todayRs 1 crore once sounded life-changing. Today, some Indians say even Rs 10-15 crore may not guarantee retirement comfort. I spoke to families to understand what they now consider their ideal retirement corpus and why the number keeps rising.

US proposes H-1B minimum wage hike, likely to make hiring foreign workers costlierThe US Department of Labour has proposed sharply higher prevailing wages for H-1B and related visa programmes. The draft rule has drawn both support and opposition ahead of the May 26 comment deadline.

Can motor insurers reject claims for reporting accidents late?Handcrafted ByOur Editors In the fifth story in our series Motor Mess, we spoke to experts to understand why delayed accident reporting often leads to insurance claim rejections, the mistakes policyholders should avoid and what options remain if such claims are rejected.

Two inspections, one pattern: Labour off the booksIn the sixth part of this series on labour laws and Inspector Raj, multiple factory inspections conducted in 1988 expose recurring patterns of undocumented labour, hidden wage records and statutory evasion.

Petrol and diesel prices likely to be hiked before May 15Petrol and diesel prices in India are likely to be hiked before May 15 as state-run oil marketing companies face rising financial stress amid a sharp surge in global crude oil prices, sources told India Today TV.

Bringing mothers in as co-applicants: Redefining home loan ownership in IndiaFamilies are now increasingly choosing to involve mothers in building long-term financial assets and securing a stronger financial future for themselves.

From flowers to financial freedom: Rethinking Mother's DayFor years, Mother's Day has been about flowers and celebrations. But slowly, conversations are shifting towards something deeper, i.e., helping mothers build savings, investments, and financial confidence of their own.

Explained: Why SBI shares tumbled nearly 7% after Q4 resultsSBI shares fell sharply after the lender's Q4 earnings disappointed the market. The reaction underscored investor concern over treasury income, margins and earnings quality.

Sensex closes over 500 points lower as SBI tumbles nearly 7% after Q4 resultsBenchmark indices closed sharply lower on Thursday after SBI's weaker-than-expected Q4 results hit banking shares. The decline reflected caution around crude oil prices, West Asia tensions and the broader earnings season.

Sensex sinks 600 points: Why is the stock market falling today?Benchmark indices fell sharply on Thursday as higher crude prices, foreign selling and weak banking stocks hit sentiment. Investors stayed cautious ahead of key Q4 results, with traders watching US-Iran tensions and oil moves.

I broke my FD 3 times: When it helped and when it hurtA fixed deposit feels safe because it is supposed to sit quietly in the background, growing slowly over time. But when life throws sudden surprises, that safety net often becomes the first thing people reach for. The real question is, when does breaking an FD actually make sense, and when does it backfire?

Think PAN is needed only for ITR Filing? 7 transactions where you must quote itPAN has quietly become one of the most important financial documents in India today, but are you aware that many banking, investment and cash transactions cannot be completed without quoting it?

entertainment.headline: Palak Tiwari says Salman Khan's Kisi Ka Bhai Kisi Ki Jaan failure didn't impact her Palak Tiwari opened up about the failure of Kisi Ka Bhai Kisi Ki Jaan, saying she never saw it as her personal setback because the film belonged entirely to Salman Khan in the public imagination. The actor also spoke about breaking stereotypes with Lukkhe and earning opportunities through auditions.

Ananya Panday-Lakshya's Chand Mera Dil trailer hints at a modern, toxic love story The trailer of Chand Mera Dil introduces Ananya Panday's Chandni and Lakshya's Aarav in a relationship marked by love, conflict and repeated apologies. The glimpses from the film hint at a modern, toxic romance.

Desi Bling trailer: Tejasswi-Karan caught in Dubai's high-society drama The trailer of Desi Bling promises glamour, luxury, and plenty of drama within Dubai's elite Indian social circle. Actors Karan Kundrra and Tejasswi Prakash's relationship takes centre stage in the show, which is set to premiere on Netflix this month.

Mohanlal reacts to Patriot criticism: I liked the movie despite mixed reactions Mohanlal said he chose Patriot because its story felt like a different experience when he first heard it. With the film receiving mixed reactions, he added that success cannot be predicted and that sincerity matters most.

Kunal Kemmu fought the thief, I cried in corner: Soha recalls burglary incident Soha Ali Khan recalled a burglary attempt at her home and said Kunal Kemmu fought the intruder. Her account reminded of how Saif Ali Khan was attacked during a robbery attempt at his home earlier this year.

Felt targeted, cornered: Sunil Pal on Samay-Ranveer episode of Kapil Sharma's show Sunil Pal claimed the audience was told not to laugh at his jokes on The Great Indian Kapil Show featuring Samay Raina and Ranveer Allahbadia. The comedian alleged his stand-up segment was dropped and accused Navjot Singh Sidhu of favouring Samay.

You're too tall: Pooja Batra recalls struggling to land film roles due to height Actor Pooja Batra revealed how her height became a major hurdle in Bollywood. She recalled doing half-splits in scenes to make her co-stars look taller. She said only Salman Khan never had a problem with her height.

Viral: Benedict Cumberbatch caught in heated road rage clash with cyclist in London Benedict Cumberbatch was filmed in a heated exchange with a cyclist on a London street after an alleged traffic incident. The undated video ended without charges but drew attention from onlookers, fans and viewers online.

Love is always louder: Trisha shuts down trolls after viral pics from Vijay oath Actor Trisha Krishnan made a statement after receiving backlash for her presence at Vijay's oath-taking ceremony on May 10. Sharing new pictures, she wrote a cryptic caption about love, which is seen as a response to those who trolled her.

Telugu actor K Bharat Kanth dies at 31 in Hyderabad road accident Telugu actor and content creator, K Bharat Kanth, and his friend cinematographer, G Sai Trilok, died in a road accident near Hyderabad's Outer Ring Road on Sunday, May 10.

Krishnavataram box office Day 4: Siddharth Gupta's devotional drama sees big jump Krishnavataram Part 1: The Heart (Hridayam) collected an estimated Rs 4.19 crore on Sunday, taking its four-day India nett total to Rs 8.77 crore. The climb came as Uttar Pradesh declared the film tax-free and ordered special screenings across districts.

Bhumi Pednekar to exit The Royals Season 2? Here's what we know Bhumi Pednekar is reportedly exiting Season 2 of The Royals, as the makers plan to shift focus from romance to the royal family and the larger palace world. Reports suggest the decision is purely story-driven, with no fallout between Bhumi and the team.

Actor Trisha mobbed after Vijay's swearing-in ceremony in Chennai. Watch Actor Trisha Krishnan attended Vijay's swearing-in ceremony in Chennai on May 10. She was mobbed when she exited amid a crush of reporters and fans, which became a major talking point of the ceremony.

BAFTA TV Awards 2026 winners full list: Owen Cooper's Adolescence wins big Owen Cooper won the BAFTA TV Award for Supporting Actor for Adolescence. The ceremony, which was held in London on May 10, saw Adolescence emerge as the night's standout winner.

Martin Short breaks silence on daughter Katherine's suicide, recalls her last words Martin Short has opened up about the death of his daughter Katherine Short, recalling her final words while calling the loss a "nightmare" for the family.

Dialogues, plots, MGR mentions: Vijay's films were trailers to political rise Vijay's films repeatedly hinted at his political ambition from before Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) was even registered as a party or in the picture. His filmography over the years has been a running prelude to power.

Dua Lipa sues Samsung: The $15 million TV box lawsuit explained Singer Dua Lipa sued Samsung for allegedly using her image on television packaging without permission. She is seeking at least $15 million in damages and claimed the company continued using the photo despite legal objections.

Rakul Preet steps out of safari vehicle, video sparks row. Gir officials react A viral video of actor Rakul Preet Singh stepping out of a safari vehicle at Gir National Park raised questions over wildlife norms. However, park officials have now clarified that no rule was broken.

Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath declares Krishnavataram Part 1 tax-free in UP The Uttar Pradesh government has granted tax-free status to Krishnavataram Part 1. Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath announced the decision on Sunday after a special screening of the film.

general news.headline: Crisis in AIADMK after Tamil Nadu polls, Shanmugam-led faction wants EPS to quitA section of AIADMK MLAs, backed by senior party leader CV Shanmugam, has asked party chief Edappadi K Palaniswami to quit due to repeated electoral defeats, sources said. The AIADMK managed to win only 47 of 234 seats in the Tamil Nadu Assembly polls.

Why PM Modi asked Indian families not to buy gold for a yearPM Modi asked families to avoid buying gold for weddings for a year. The appeal linked rising oil prices and gold imports to pressure on the rupee and foreign exchange reserves.

140 questions in NEET practice bank match actual exam, Rajasthan cops probeConcerns over a possible NEET 2026 paper leak have grown after investigators found that a handwritten "guess paper" circulated before the exam allegedly matched questions worth nearly 600 marks in the actual NEET paper held on May 3. The NTA, which conducts NEET, said it was aware of the alleged malpractice and awaiting the probe outcome.

Even Kerala BJP has a preference for Congress's CM faceAmid an intense Congress power struggle over Kerala's next Chief Minister, Kerala BJP chief Rajeev Chandrasekhar appeared to jokingly hint at the BJP's preference.

The math behind PM Modi's gold, petrol and foreign travel advisoryPM Modi's latest advisory is not just about cutting fuel use or avoiding gold purchases. It is really about India's current account deficit, rising crude oil prices and protecting the economy from external shocks. Here is why your fuel bill, gold jewellery and foreign trip suddenly matter more than you think.

Love is always louder: Trisha shuts down trolls after viral pics from Vijay oathActor Trisha Krishnan made a statement after receiving backlash for her presence at Vijay's oath-taking ceremony on May 10. Sharing new pictures, she wrote a cryptic caption about love, which is seen as a response to those who trolled her.

Hardik Pandya unfollows MI on Insta as fallout rumours growIPL 2026: Hardik Pandya briefly appeared to unfollow Mumbai Indians on Instagram after MI's playoff exit. The account returned within minutes, but the episode intensified speculation around his future.

Not a victim, knew Sukesh's past and still accepted gifts: ED on Jacqueline's pleaThe Enforcement Directorate opposed Jacqueline Fernandez's plea to turn approver in the Sukesh Chandrashekhar money laundering case. The agency told a Delhi court there was sufficient material against her and sought time for a detailed response.

Rs 5 crore, Rs 12 crore, Rs 40 crore? What retirement means for Indians todayOnce upon a time, Rs 1 crore felt like enough to secure life after retirement. Today, many Indians believe even Rs 10–15 crore may not be enough. I spoke to families to find out what retirement means to them today, and why expectations are rising.

Anthropic, OpenAI execs meet Hindu and Sikh religious leaders to discuss AI ethicsAnthropic and OpenAI executives reportedly met Hindu and Sikh religious leaders in New York as part of a roundtable to discuss how to instil moral values in AI. Here is what happened.

Norovirus outbreak hits Caribbean cruise, over 100 fall illA norovirus outbreak on a Caribbean cruise ship left more than 100 passengers and crew members ill during a voyage from Florida. The episode has renewed focus on how fast stomach infections can spread in crowded travel settings and on the need for strict hygiene at sea.

Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath declares Krishnavataram Part 1 tax-free in UPThe Uttar Pradesh government has granted tax-free status to Krishnavataram Part 1. Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath announced the decision on Sunday after a special screening of the film.

IPL 2026: Did Mumbai Indians bring back the wrong Pandya?IPL 2026: While Hardik Pandya has been captaining a sinking ship at Mumbai Indians, his older brother Krunal is quietly, steadily becoming the most indispensable match winner at Royal Challengers Bengaluru.

Live: Class 12th results to be out soonCBSE Board 12th Results Live Updates: The CBSE Class 12 Result 2026 is expected to be declared shortly, with over 18 lakh students eagerly awaiting their board exam scores. Students can check their CBSE 12th results online at the CBSE results official website -- results.cbse.nic.in and download their digital marksheet through DigiLocker, where a "Results Coming Soon" banner has already appeared. Get live updates on CBSE Class 12 Result 2026 date, time, direct result link, and DigiLocker marksheet.

BSF to get full control of border fencing in 45 days: Bengal CM at 1st Cabinet meetWest Bengal Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari said his first Cabinet had decided to transfer border fencing land to the BSF within 45 days. The move fulfils a key BJP poll promise and came alongside approvals for pending central schemes in the state.

Sensex crashes 1,000 points: Why is the stock market falling today?The selloff on Dalal Street was broad-based across sectors, with banking, aviation, jewellery and consumption-linked stocks witnessing heavy pressure amid concerns over rising crude oil prices and their impact on India's economy.

No sunset in this city for the next 84 days. Where is it and why?Utqiagvik has entered its Midnight Sun period after the season's final sunset on Saturday. The 84-day stretch of continuous daylight reflects the extreme seasonal shifts caused by Earth's axial tilt.

'Common man' Vijay swaps veshti-shirt for blazer and pants at oathVijay's attire and messaging marked a political turning point in Tamil Nadu, symbolising a generational shift and the end of an era of Dravidian political norms. With Arvind Kejriwal's "aam aadmi" image recently under strain amid controversies, Vijay appears to have filled the vacuum.

What is a NEET 'guess paper', and why are these prediction sheets controversial?Every NEET season, "guess papers" flood coaching groups and student chats, promising to predict the real exam. Most are seen as smart prediction material, until the overlap with the actual paper becomes uncomfortably high. That is when one of India's most controversial exam-season practices comes under scrutiny again.

Calcutta was the OG aviation hub long before Dubai's riseDubai's aviation hub faces an existential threat from the war in the Middle East. But what many don't know is that before Dubai's rise, India's Calcutta (now Kolkata) was a major global aviation hub. Till the 1960s, Dum Dum Airport was served by almost every major airline to the Americas and continental Europe. Today, not a single direct flight connects to anywhere beyond Southeast Asia.

Can getting periods once in three months help women stay fertile longer?Chinese biologist Hongmei Wang is studying whether less frequent periods can preserve healthy eggs for longer. The work points to new fertility options but also raises questions about oestrogen's role in overall health.

Madhya Pradesh CM releases 2 Botswana cheetahs into Kuno National Park open forestMadhya Pradesh CM Mohan Yadav released two female cheetahs from Botswana into Kuno National Park's open forest on Monday. Officials and experts said the move strengthens Project Cheetah and improves the population's genetic diversity.

UPI payment at toll booth provides lead in killing of Suvendu Adhikari's aidePolice have made no arrests more than four days after Chandranath Rath was shot dead near his Madhyamgram home. Investigators believe the killing was a planned operation, with clues pointing to outside shooters and local support.

Iran Foreign Minister to visit India this week amid Gulf war, BRICS meet on agendaThe BRICS Foreign Ministers' meeting will serve as a precursor to the 18th BRICS Summit scheduled in New Delhi this September, where leaders of major emerging economies are expected to meet amid rising geopolitical tensions and growing global uncertainty.

Palak Tiwari says Salman Khan's Kisi Ka Bhai Kisi Ki Jaan failure didn't impact herPalak Tiwari opened up about the failure of Kisi Ka Bhai Kisi Ki Jaan, saying she never saw it as her personal setback because the film belonged entirely to Salman Khan in the public imagination. The actor also spoke about breaking stereotypes with Lukkhe and earning opportunities through auditions.

Ananya Panday-Lakshya's Chand Mera Dil trailer hints at a modern, toxic love storyThe trailer of Chand Mera Dil introduces Ananya Panday's Chandni and Lakshya's Aarav in a relationship marked by love, conflict and repeated apologies. The glimpses from the film hint at a modern, toxic romance.

sports.headline: Why are all cricket finals going to Ahmedabad? The BCCI announced the venue for the Indian Premier League 2026 final. No prizes for guessing, that the match was granted to the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad, much to the dissatisfaction of many fans.

Krunal excited about Kohli's slump? RCB star explains bizarre statement Krunal Pandya dismissed concerns over Virat Kohli's two-game run drought and backed him to hit back hard. Krunal said that there is no worry in the RCB camp about Kohli's form.

Peter Schmeichel accuses Arsenal of hypocrisy after controversial VAR call Peter Schmeichel blasted the officials after Arsenal's controversial 1-0 win over West Ham United, claiming the league leaders "would never be top" if similar physical challenges were consistently punished as fouls.

Delhi Capitals accept defeat in play-off race, to act party poopers for others IPL 2026: Ian Bell says Delhi Capitals want to finish strongly despite an inconsistent season damaging their playoff push. Delhi will be aiming to spoil the party for the rest of the teams as the season comes to a close.

Shahbaz Ahmed leads big-money buys at Bengal T20 League auction Shahbaz Ahmed emerged as the biggest buy at the Bengal T20 League Men's Auction after Shrachi Tribes Rarh Tigers retained the Indian all-rounder for INR 12.20 lakh through the Right To Match card.

How MS Dhoni's advice turned CSK's Urvil Patel into an IPL record breaker Urvil Patel said a dressing-room conversation with MS Dhoni helped him reset before his 13-ball fifty against LSG. The knock powered CSK past LSG and doubled as a tribute to his father, who told him to bat freely.

No IPL stars in Australia squad for Pakistan ODIs, some return for Bangladesh tour Cricket Australia named separate white-ball squads for Pakistan and Bangladesh under Mitchell Marsh. Pat Cummins, Mitchell Starc and Josh Hazlewood will miss both the tours, which get underway from May 30.

PBKS vs DC preview: Can Punjab arrest their slide in the hills? PBKS vs DC preview, IPL 2026: With three straight losses and their season on the brink, Punjab Kings seek a mountain-air reset in Dharamshala. They face a demoralised Delhi Capitals, desperate to play the spoiler and salvage some tattered pride.

IPL 2026: Did Mumbai Indians bring back the wrong Pandya? IPL 2026: While Hardik Pandya has been captaining a sinking ship at Mumbai Indians, his older brother Krunal is quietly, steadily becoming the most indispensable match winner at Royal Challengers Bengaluru.

RCB coach Flower given hefty fine for fiery umpire confrontation during MI clash Andy Flower was slapped with a hefty fine after he confronted the fourth umpire after a disputed boundary call during RCB's win over MI. The fine settled a Level 1 code breach, but the flashpoint underlined the strain around high-stakes decisions.

Did David give MI middle finger salute in Raipur tie? RCB star's viral video A video appearing to show Tim David making an obscene gesture after RCB's win over MI has gone viral. RCB were able to edge past MI to steal the win in Raipur and move to the top of the table.

Bring back Bhuvneshwar Kumar: World Cup winners call for RCB star's India returnBhuvneshwar Kumar's four wickets and a late six in RCB's win over Mumbai Indians triggered fresh calls for an India recall from Kris Srikkanth and R Ashwin. His control with the new ball and at the death has turned a strong IPL season into a serious selection argument.

Emotional Flick fights back tears after El Clasico win, mourns father's deathAn emotional Hansi Flick held back tears after El Clasico win over Real Madrid, revealing that his father had passed away on the eve of the game. Barca defeated Madrid 2-0 to clinch the LaLiga title.

IPL 2026 Play of the Day: Bhuvneshwar's six trumps his four in Raipur nail-biterIPL 2026: Bhuvneshwar Kumar's four-wicket haul had already put RCB in control against Mumbai Indians in Raipur. But it was his pressure six in the final over that ultimately decided the May 10 thriller and sealed a dramatic two-wicket win.

I don't think we deserved to win: Patidar after RCB win last-ball thriller vs MIIPL 2026: Rajat Patidar admitted Royal Challengers Bengaluru made hard work of a chase they should have controlled against Mumbai Indians in Raipur. Krunal Pandya's rescue and Bhuvneshwar Kumar's late blow sent RCB top and ended Mumbai's playoff hopes.

Krunal Pandya special knocks MI out as RCB win epic last-ball thrillerRoyal Challengers Bengaluru dethroned SunRisers Hyderabad from the top of the IPL points table. It did not come easy as RCB were stretched to the final ball of the match, where they eventually clinched a low-scoring thriller vs MI.

Hardik Pandya unfollows MI on Insta as fallout rumours growIPL 2026: Hardik Pandya briefly appeared to unfollow Mumbai Indians on Instagram after MI's playoff exit. The account returned within minutes, but the episode intensified speculation around his future.

Krunal Pandya tears up remembering good old MI days with Pollard and HardikKrunal Pandya made 73 through cramps to steer Royal Challengers Bengaluru past Mumbai Indians in a final-ball chase. The innings knocked Mumbai out of the IPL 2026 playoff race and brought back memories of his bond with Hardik Pandya and Kieron Pollard.

Controversial VAR tilts Premier League title race in Arsenal's favourArsenal beat West Ham after VAR ruled out Callum Wilson's stoppage-time equaliser for contact on David Raya. The decision inflamed accusations of inconsistency and sharpened pressure at both ends of the table.

India U17 women eye historic World Cup dream in Asian Cup quarterfinal vs ChinaIndia's U17 women face hosts China in the AFC U17 Women's Asian Cup quarterfinal on Monday. A win would send India to the 2026 FIFA U17 Women's World Cup in Morocco through qualification for the first time.

Virat Kohli milestone IPL night ends in golden duck heartbreakIPL 2026: Virat Kohli's 278th IPL appearance ended with a first-ball duck as he miscued Deepak Chahar to mid-off against Mumbai Indians. The failure made it successive ducks for Kohli, a rare slump on a night that should have marked a milestone.

Despite ban, did LSG support staff use phone in dugout during CSK clash?IPL 2026: Broadcast visuals during the Lucknow Super Giants-Chennai Super Kings match appeared to show a person using a phone near the LSG camp. With IPL rules tightly restricting phone use during live play, the viral clip has sparked questions without any official clarification.

Rishabh Pant gives bizarre reason for major blunder in defeat vs CSK at ChepaukIPL 2026: Rishabh Pant defended handing Aiden Markram the final over as Chennai Super Kings chased 204. The call exposed Lucknow's muddled death-overs planning and ended Chennai's seven-year 180-plus chase drought.

Virat Kohli pauses training in Raipur to watch Urvil Patel's six-hitting barrageRCB vs MI: Urvil Patel's breathtaking knock against Lucknow Super Giants caught the attention of everyone on Sunday. Even Virat Kohli paused his warm-up routine in Raipur and remained glued to the TV screen as Urvil smashed the joint-fastest fifty in IPL history.

Vijay, at last: Urvil Patel powers CSK's first 180-plus chase in 8 yearsIPL 2026, CSK vs LSG: Josh Inglis threatened 220. Jamie Overton pulled CSK back. Then Urvil Patel took over – and Chennai finally got the Vijay they had been waiting eight years for.

Why Hardik Pandya is missing RCB vs MI? Mumbai explain captain's absenceHardik Pandya missed Mumbai Indians' IPL 2026 match against Royal Challengers Bengaluru after failing to get medical clearance. His continued absence left Suryakumar Yadav leading MI as their playoff hopes stayed on the line.

Urvil Patel hits IPL's joint-fastest fifty, celebrates with note for papaIPL 2026: Urvil Patel produced one of the most explosive knocks in IPL history on Sunday night at Chepauk. The CSK batter smashed a 13-ball fifty against LSG before celebrating the milestone with an emotional note dedicated to his father.

I'll hopefully be there: Mitchell Starc eyes 2027 ODI World CupMitchell Starc says he wants a few more years in international cricket and has not ruled out the 2027 World Cup. After retiring from T20Is, he is trying to manage his workload carefully.

1st Test: Rizwan fifty, Azan Awais hundred not enough as Bangladesh take leadBAN vs PAK, 1st Test: Azan Awais marked his debut with a century, but Pakistan conceded a 26-run first innings lead. Mehidy Hasan Miraz's five-wicket haul kept the hosts in control as the Mirpur pitch worsened steadily.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
